result:
All changes in place. I left the work uncommitted since neither the task nor my guidelines call for committing without an explicit request.

I made the static 5-step triage in `start.mdx` **interactive** — the single highest-leverage, mission-aligned change, since guided decision support is exactly what separates a real product from a typical administration website. The new assistant reproduces the existing decision tree exactly, so it introduces no new legal claims, and the full static flow remains below as the authoritative reference and no-JS fallback.

SUMMARY:
- New `components/clasificador.tsx` (`<Clasificador />`, `'use client'`): a state-machine triage mirroring `start.mdx` exactly — P1 ámbito (Art. 2) → P2 prohibida (Art. 5) → P3 alto riesgo (Art. 6, Anexo I/III) → P4 transparencia (Art. 50) → P5 rol (Art. 3). Yields 9 outcomes (fuera de ámbito, GPAI, prohibido, transparencia, mínimo, and alto riesgo × proveedor/sector público/desplegador/intermediario), each with summary, the applicable application date derived from the chosen path, caveat notes, and action buttons to the matching itinerario + key guide. Accessible (`role="group"`/`aria-labelledby`, `role="status"`/`aria-live`, keyboard-operable, focus rings) and styled with native Fumadocs tokens; includes Back / Restart.
- `components/mdx.tsx`: registered `Clasificador`.
- `content/docs/start.mdx`: inserted `<Clasificador />` under a new "Clasifícate en menos de un minuto" heading; kept the static triage intact below as "El recorrido en detalle"; renamed one heading (no inbound links to the old anchor).
- `.hermes/overnight/progress.md`: logged this pass (Pasada 5) and back-filled the previously unlogged guides-index pass (commit 7e8fed3), noting its leftover metadata-duplication debt.

VALIDATION:
- `npm run build`: success — compiles, TypeScript passes (no `ignoreBuildErrors`), 78 static pages (was 75).
- Exported HTML: assistant's initial state (heading + first question + options) renders into `out/docs/start/index.html`.
- Links: all 5 itinerary anchor targets exist in `out/docs/itinerarios/index.html`; all 6 destination pages (guides 01/03/06/08, official-sources, itinerarios) export. 0 broken links.
- Fidelity: every question/option/date taken verbatim from already-verified `start.mdx` content — no new regulatory claims.

NEXT_IDEA:
Close the Pass-4 debt: refactor `GuiaResumen` to take just `n="05"` and read from `lib/guides-meta.ts` via `guiasPorNumero`, removing the duplicated inline metadata in all 16 guide MDX files so the "single source of truth" actually becomes single (low risk, removes drift between the guides index and each guide's "De un vistazo" header).
